event.summary: Recorded passing validation; acceptance remains a separate explicit transition.

acceptance.proof: The observation binds the exact official package/library identities and digests used previously without inspecting implementation source.
At least two bounded synthetic cases show LSLC-002P sends its accepted query and admits the official response envelope/document on loopback with exact typed outcomes.
Only public-safe derived structure and pass/fail facts are committed; raw packets, XML, endpoints, diagnostics, binaries, environments, and caches remain private external evidence.
The public record explicitly does not claim responder direction, multicast/interface behavior, reachability, currentness, performance, or ecosystem interoperability.

document.commit_policy: Commit only sanitized derived evidence and routing after the private black-box run; keep raw artifacts external and lifecycle/acceptance/push commits separate.

instruction_surfaces.change_reason: Route the one-direction official loopback observation and its strict nonclaims.
instruction_surfaces.validation: Run the observation provenance/privacy and rolling owner gates.

instruction_surfaces.change_reason: Separate observed Rust-client compatibility from unproved responder and network behavior.
Record exact direction, endpoint provenance, limitations, and evidence role.
Bind the official package/library provenance without implementation-source intake.
instruction_surfaces.validation: Run the provenance and public-boundary gates.

instruction_surfaces.change_reason: Route bounded black-box replay validation and private raw-evidence handling.
instruction_surfaces.validation: Run the observation and workflow gates.

instruction_surfaces.change_reason: Synchronize existing Rusty LSL black-box/public-private guidance; no shared-skill content change expected.
Synchronize existing behavioral-evidence and limitation guidance; no shared-skill content change expected.

non_scope: Official-client to Rust-responder direction, multicast/broadcast discovery, interface enumeration, endpoint selection, non-loopback networking, reachability, multiple hosts, or ecosystem-wide interoperability.

Implementation-source inspection, copied code, raw packet/XML publication, endpoint/host/environment values, timing/performance claims, correlation/currentness, retries, clocks, outlet/inlet/sample behavior, or runtime changes.
Dependencies, Cargo features, unsafe, FFI, devices, private artifacts in the repository, or Manifold authority.
document.objective: Observe the accepted LSLC-002P Rust client performing one bounded loopback short-info discovery exchange against the exact pinned official protocol-110 endpoint, publishing only sanitized derived pass/fail and contract-admission evidence while retaining raw packets, endpoint values, diagnostics, binaries, environments, and caches privately.
outputs: Public-safe derived observation matrix for Rust-client to official-responder loopback direction.
Pinned provenance/privacy/limitation checker and synchronized compatibility documentation.
Private external raw observation bundle retained outside the repository.
